title: Windows podman WSL2 Temporary failure in name resolution proxy
body:
### Issue Description


podman desktop on the Windows host gives the same issue. As does podman cli from windows.

Scoured around the issues on here, and ran
[user@BN405065 ~]$ cat /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh
export http_proxy="http://companyproxy.com:3142"
export HTTP_PROXY="http://companyproxy.com:3142"

I tried updating to the latest source, downloaded the windows msi installer. While podman desktop does say "4.4.2" the latest...versions seems to have a bit more going on, so i'm not sure how this all interrelates

PS C:\Users\sreich> podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.2
API Version:  4.4.2
Go Version:   go1.19.5
Git Commit:   74afe26887f814d1c39925a1624851ef3590e79c
Built:        Thu Feb 23 15:02:02 2023
OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Nov 11 10:24:13 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


### Steps to reproduce the issue

install podman desktop, WSL2, possibly be behind a corporate firewall and require a proxy (but it seems like the failure is not even getting as far as leaving my machine)

From within wsl's podman-machine-default, I try this:
[user@BN405065 ~]$ podman pull fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: pinging container registry registry.fedoraproject.org: Get "https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: Temporary failure in name resolution

also ran this and rebooted the machine
 $env:http_proxy="http://mycompanyproxy.com:3142"

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received[user@BN405065 ~]$ podman pull fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: pinging container registry registry.fedoraproject.org: Get "https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: Temporary failure in name resolution


getting this in podman desktop and powershell's podman, and within the podman WSL default container

### Describe the results you expected

image pull success

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10, podman desktop, WSL2. Corporate firewall/vpn. Although I have connected directly which should bypass the VPN. Attempting to use proxy though, still same issue

### Additional information

when starting podman desktop sometimes i get the below. unsure if related.

⚠️ Docker Socket Compatibility: Podman is not emulating the default Docker socket path: //./pipe/docker_engine. Docker-specific tools may not work.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I’m assuming that “temporary failure in name resolution” is actually expected if the proxy is not being used; is that the case?

---

> ````
> [user@BN405065 ~]$ podman info
> ```

> ```
> [user@BN405065 ~]$ cat /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh`
> ```

So `BN405065` is the remote client? And the proxy variables are on the client? Are they set on the remote server as well?

---

A quick search of the Podman codebase suggests that for `podman machine`, the proxy variables as set in the `podman machine start`’s environment are expected to be propagated into the VM. I don’t know what that means for Podman Desktop; my first guess is that these might need to be Windows environment variables, not UNIX-only WSL environment variables — but don’t take my word for it.

@ashley-cui is the authoritative expert.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Because we heard no feedback, closing...
